# Denver Carlos Snuffer Jr. (Context Asset)

Source URL
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Denver_Snuffer_Jr.

Source snapshot date
- 2026-07-23 (source page indicates last edit on 2026-06-13)

## Purpose in this repository
This profile is a neutral background context asset for AI crew prompts when discussing restoration-adjacent movements, publications, and modern Latter-day Saint history topics.

## Identity and background (high-level)
- Denver Carlos Snuffer Jr. is an American attorney, author, and lecturer.
- He was raised in a Baptist household and later converted to the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints.
- He earned higher education credentials including a J.D. from BYU's J. Reuben Clark Law School.
- Public-facing work includes legal practice, long-form religious writing, lectures, and blog/paper publication.

## Latter-day Saint context and disciplinary history
- Snuffer published books and essays critical of institutional trajectories in modern LDS history.
- His 2011 book Passing the Heavenly Gift drew significant public and ecclesiastical attention.
- In 2013, he was excommunicated from the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints following dispute over publication and related teachings.

## Relationship to fellowships/remnant movement
- After 2013, Snuffer became a central reference figure for a loosely organized network commonly described as remnant/fellowship communities.
- These groups are generally low-hierarchy and fellowship-based rather than a centralized denomination.
- Public reporting describes movement participants as often current or former Latter-day Saints.
- Some conference settings associated with these fellowships have treated portions of his teachings as scripture or canon within their own community practice.

## Publications (selected, non-exhaustive)
- The Second Comforter: Conversing with the Lord Through the Veil (2006)
- Nephi's Isaiah (2006)
- Eighteen Verses: A Discussion of the Book of Mormon (2007)
- Passing the Heavenly Gift (2011)
- Remembering the Covenant (2013)
- Preserving the Restoration (2015)
- A Man Without Doubt (2016)

## Usage guidance for AI crew
- Treat this profile as orientation context, not as sole authority.
- Distinguish clearly between:
  - biographical facts,
  - Snuffer's own claims,
  - third-party commentary about his movement.
- Prefer neutral wording; avoid polemical framing.
- For sensitive or disputed claims, cite source and attribution language such as "according to".
- When doctrinal comparisons are requested, present differences descriptively and avoid adjudicating truth claims.

## Citation note
This document is a paraphrased context summary derived from the listed source URL and is intended for prompt grounding within this repository.
